ucs.title: [LLM] APT18 (Dynamite Panda) legacy at.exe scheduled-task persistence chained to cmd.exe payload
ucs.description: Hunts the deprecated `at.exe` Windows scheduling utility being used to register a job that subsequently spawns a Windows command interpreter or scripting host. APT18 / TG-0416 is documented (MITRE G0026 — T1053.002 + T1059.003) to lean on the legacy `at` command rather than `schtasks.exe` for persistence and timed execution — an idiomatic Chinese-state TTP that survives because `at.exe` is largely abandoned by legitimate admins on modern Windows.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="at.exe" AND (match(Processes.process,"(?i)\b\d{1,2}:\d{2}\b") OR Processes.process IN ("*/interactive*","*/every:*","*/next:*"))) OR (Processes.parent_process_name="at.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe")) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT match(user,"\$$") | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 30d;
// (1) Direct at.exe scheduling — schedule-time argument, /interactive flag, or /every:/next: switches
let AtUsage = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName =~ "at.exe"
    | where InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\"     // legit on-disk at.exe only
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b\d{1,2}:\d{2}\b"
         or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/interactive","/every:","/next:");
// (2) Children spawned by an at-registered job (parent = at.exe)
let AtChildren = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "at.exe"
    | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe");
union AtUsage, AtChildren
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IsRemoteSession = AdditionalFields
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 30d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688                       // process create — requires audit policy + cmdline GPO
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where (NewProcessName endswith "\\at.exe"
          and (CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b\d{1,2}:\d{2}\b"
               or CommandLine has_any ("/interactive","/every:","/next:")))
     or (ParentProcessName endswith "\\at.exe"
          and NewProcessName matches regex @"(?i)\\(cmd|powershell|pwsh|cscript|wscript|mshta|rundll32|regsvr32)\.exe$")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, LogonId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT18 (Dynamite Panda) Legacy at.exe Scheduled Task Abuse
id: 9c3d2f5b-1a44-4f1c-b6c3-1a2e7d4f8b21
status: experimental
description: Detects use of the deprecated `at.exe` scheduling utility (or processes spawned by it) — a tradecraft signature of APT18 / TG-0416 / Dynamite Panda for timed execution and persistence on Windows hosts.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0026/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1053/002/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1053.002
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.g0026
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection_at_schedule:
    Image|endswith: '\at.exe'
  selection_at_args:
    - CommandLine|re: '(?i)\b\d{1,2}:\d{2}\b'
    - CommandLine|contains:
        - '/interactive'
        - '/every:'
        - '/next:'
  selection_at_child:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\at.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
  filter_machine_account:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: ((selection_at_schedule and selection_at_args) or selection_at_child) and not filter_machine_account
falsepositives:
  - Legacy administrator scripts on pre-Windows-10 hosts
  - Backup or monitoring software still scripted around at.exe
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT18 is documented in MITRE G0026 as one of the few well-tracked clusters that explicitly uses T1053.002 (`at` command) over T1053.005 (`schtasks`). On modern Windows, `at.exe` was deprecated in Windows 8 / Server 2012 and is essentially absent from legitimate admin workflows — so `at.exe` invoked with a HH:MM schedule, /interactive, /every: or /next:, OR `at.exe` parenting cmd.exe / scripting ho

ucs.title: [LLM] APT18 dropper-in-user-path → Run-key persistence → external HTTP beacon (HTTPBrowser/Gh0st chain)
ucs.description: Correlates three APT18 staples within a one-hour window on the same host: (a) a binary executing from a user-writable path (\AppData\, \ProgramData\, \Windows\Temp\, \Public\), (b) that same process — or a sibling under the same path — writing a Run/RunOnce value, and (c) outbound traffic to a public IP. APT18's HTTPBrowser and Gh0st-derived RATs (T1547.001 + T1071.001 + T1105) historically drop into user-writable directories, register Run-key autostart, and beacon over HTTP/HTTPS to commodity-hosted C2.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as persistTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path IN ("*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*","*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce*") AND match(Registry.registry_value_data,"(?i)(\\Users\\[^\\\\]+\\AppData\\|\\ProgramData\\|\\Windows\\Temp\\|\\Public\\)") AND Registry.process_name!="msiexec.exe" AND Registry.process_name!="explorer.exe" by host Registry.user Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_name Registry.registry_value_data Registry.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | where NOT match(user,"\$$") | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as dest_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(All_Traffic.app) as c2_url from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_category!="private" AND match(All_Traffic.process_path,"(?i)\\Users\\[^\\\\]+\\AppData\\|\\ProgramData\\|\\Windows\\Temp\\|\\Public\\") by host All_Traffic.process_name All_Traffic.process_path _time | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename _time as netTime] | where abs(netTime - persistTime) <= 3600 | table host user registry_path registry_value_data process_name dest_ip dest_port c2_url persistTime netTime
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let WindowMin = 60;
let UserWritable = @"(?i)\\Users\\[^\\]+\\AppData\\|\\ProgramData\\|\\Windows\\Temp\\|\\Public\\";
// (1) Run/RunOnce value pointing into a user-writable path, set by a non-installer process
let RunKeyDrops = DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType == "RegistryValueSet"
    | where RegistryKey has_any (@"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run",
                                  @"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce")
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","explorer.exe","setup.exe","installer.exe","trustedinstaller.exe")
    | where RegistryValueData matches regex UserWritable
    | project PersistTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              PersistUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              PersistParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData;
// (2) Same host: process running from user-writable path beaconing to public IP
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath matches regex UserWritable
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner RunKeyDrops on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (PersistTime - WindowMin*1m .. PersistTime + WindowMin*1m)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, PersistUser,
          BeaconProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          BeaconPath    = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          BeaconCmd     = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData,
          PersistTime,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, PersistTime)
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let WindowMin = 60;
let UserWritable = @"(?i)\\Users\\[^\\]+\\AppData\\|\\ProgramData\\|\\Windows\\Temp\\|\\Public\\";
let RunKeyDrops = ImRegistryEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has_any (@"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run",
                                  @"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce")
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | where ActingProcessName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","explorer.exe","setup.exe","installer.exe","trustedinstaller.exe")
    | where RegistryValueData matches regex UserWritable
    | project PersistTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              ActingProcessName, RegistryKey, RegistryValue, RegistryValueData;
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where NetworkDirection =~ "Outbound" or isempty(NetworkDirection)
| where not(ipv4_is_private(DstIpAddr))
| where ActingProcessCommandLine matches regex UserWritable
   or ActingProcessName matches regex UserWritable
| join kind=inner RunKeyDrops on $left.DvcHostname == $right.DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (PersistTime - WindowMin*1m .. PersistTime + WindowMin*1m)
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          BeaconProcess = ActingProcessName,
          BeaconCmd     = ActingProcessCommandLine,
          DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber, Url,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValue, RegistryValueData, PersistTime,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, PersistTime)
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: This is the canonical APT18/Dynamite Panda chain documented in CrowdStrike and Dell SecureWorks reporting on TG-0416: drop a Gh0st/HTTPBrowser-derived implant into AppData/ProgramData/Temp, hook it into HKCU/HKLM Run for autostart (T1547.001), then HTTP-beacon to public infrastructure (T1071.001 + T1105). Each pillar individually is noisy (any installer touches Run keys, any service connects to pu
